conformance.risky_behavior: Server rolls the equipped weapon profile against the authoritative target and resolves bounded nonlethal harm.

conformance.cosyworld_delta: Ordinary room travel remains a movement operation.
Bounded Escape remains an operation until engagement consequences exist.

conformance.safe_behavior: Defend outside combat is unavailable unless an authored project maps it to preparation.
conformance.risky_behavior: The actor spends the encounter turn to impose the bounded dodging condition.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Uses the bounded encounter condition rather than the complete SRD duration model.

conformance.safe_behavior: Advances only an authored active project or bond and names its recipient.
conformance.risky_behavior: Repeated unprepared assistance can apply the authored tired consequence.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Help is a journaled project contribution, never a self-stacking generic bonus.
No profile-1 hidden-state resolver is active.

conformance.safe_behavior: Ordinary greetings remain free Chat and never create this action.
conformance.risky_behavior: A deterministic Charisma check commits one of the authored cooperation outcomes before narration.
legal_targets: nearby active resident with contextual offer
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Only bounded authored cooperation changes use Influence.

conformance.safe_behavior: Unsupported or unprepared spell content produces no offer.
conformance.risky_behavior: The kernel validates target and prepared instance, consumes one use, and moves the card to Exhausted.
legal_targets: effect-declared active actor in the same location
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Only validated prepared Item-card effects are executable.

conformance.safe_behavior: Creates one scoped setup only when an authored project can consume it.
conformance.risky_behavior: The setup has explicit duration and is consumed by the next eligible contribution.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Prepare is a bounded one-use project setup rather than a general reaction trigger.

conformance.safe_behavior: Authored safe perceptual truths resolve without a roll and remain learn-once.
conformance.risky_behavior: Authored risky perception uses the deterministic Wisdom check path.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Listen and Notice are labels for contextual Search, not separate mechanics.

conformance.safe_behavior: Authored safe analytical truths resolve without a roll and remain learn-once.
conformance.risky_behavior: Authored risky analysis uses the deterministic Intelligence check path.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Study is distinct from perceptual Search and may be independently reskinned.

conformance.safe_behavior: Requires a validated usable item or authored feature contract.
conformance.risky_behavior: Authoritative uses, exhaustion, targets, clock effects, and claim keys are resolved once.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Use and Work are contextual labels over validated Utilize bindings.

conditions.name: Blinded
conditions.source_text: A blinded creature can’t see and automatically fails any ability check that requires sight. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and the creature’s attack rolls have disadvantage.

conditions.name: Charmed
conditions.source_text: A charmed creature can’t attack the charmer or target the charmer with harmful abilities or magical effects. The charmer has advantage on any ability check to interact socially with the creature.

conditions.name: Deafened
conditions.source_text: A deafened creature can’t hear and automatically fails any ability check that requires hearing.

conditions.name: Exhaustion
conditions.source_text: Some special abilities and environmental hazards, such as starvation and the long-term effects of freezing or scorching temperatures, can lead to a special condition called exhaustion. Exhaustion is measured in six levels. An effect can give a creature one or more levels of exhaustion, as specified in the effect’s description. Level Effect 1 Disadvantage on ability checks 2 Speed halved 3 Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws 4 Hit point maximum halved 5 Speed reduced to 0 6 Death. If an already exhausted creature suffers another effect that causes exhaustion, its current level of exhaustion increases by the amount specified in the effect’s description. A creature suffers the effect of its current level of exhaustion as well as all lower levels. For example, a creature suffering level 2 exhaustion has its speed halved and has disadvantage on ability checks. An effect that removes exhaustion reduces its level as specified in the effect’s description, with all exhaustion effects ending if a creature’s exhaustion level is reduced below 1. Finishing a long rest reduces a creature’s exhaustion level by 1, provided that the creature has also ingested some food and drink.

conditions.name: Frightened
conditions.source_text: A frightened creature has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of its fear is within line of sight. The creature can’t willingly move closer to the source of its fear.

conditions.name: Grappled
conditions.source_text: A grappled creature’s speed becomes 0, and it can’t benefit from any bonus to its speed. The condition ends if the grappler is incapacitated (see the condition). The condition also ends if an effect removes the grappled creature from the reach of the grappler or grappling effect, such as when a creature is hurled away by the thunderwave spell.

conditions.name: Incapacitated
conditions.source_text: An incapacitated creature can’t take actions or reactions.

conditions.name: Invisible
conditions.source_text: An invisible creature is impossible to see without the aid of magic or a special sense. For the purpose of hiding, the creature is heavily obscured. The creature’s location can be detected by any noise it makes or any tracks it leaves. Attack rolls against the creature have disadvantage, and the creature’s attack rolls have advantage.

conditions.name: Paralyzed
conditions.source_text: A paralyzed creature is incapacitated (see the condition) and can’t move or speak. The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage. Any attack that hits the creature is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of the creature.

conditions.name: Petrified
conditions.source_text: A petrified creature is transformed, along with any nonmagical object it is wearing or carrying, into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone). Its weight increases by a factor of ten, and it ceases aging. The creature is incapacitated (see the condition), can’t move or speak, and is unaware of its surroundings. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage. The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws. The creature has resistance to all damage. The creature is immune to poison and disease,although a poison or disease already in its system is suspended, not neutralized.

conditions.name: Poisoned
conditions.source_text: A poisoned creature has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

conditions.name: Prone
conditions.source_text: A prone creature’s only movement option is to crawl, unless it stands up and thereby ends the condition. The creature has disadvantage on attack rolls. An attack roll against the creature has advantage if the attacker is within 5 feet of the creature.Otherwise, the attack roll has disadvantage.

conditions.name: Restrained
conditions.source_text: A restrained creature’s speed becomes 0, and it can’t benefit from any bonus to its speed. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and the creature’s attack rolls have disadvantage. The creature has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

conditions.name: Stunned
conditions.source_text: A stunned creature is incapacitated (see the condition), can’t move, and can speak onlyfalteringly. The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.

conditions.name: Unconscious
conditions.source_text: An unconscious creature is incapacitated (see the condition), can’t move or speak, and is unaware ofits surroundings The creature drops whatever it’s holding and falls prone. The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage. Any attack that hits the creature is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of the creature.

features.name: Innate Spellcasting.
features.description: The dryad’s innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 14). The dryad can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components: At will: druidcraft 3/day each: entangle, goodberry; 1/day each: barkskin, pass without trace, shillelagh

features.name: Magic Resistance.
features.description: The dryad has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

features.name: Speak with Beasts and Plants.
features.description: The dryad can communicate with beasts and plants as if they shared a language.

features.name: Tree Stride.
features.description: Once on her turn, the dryad can use 10 feet of her movement to step magically into one living tree within her reach and emerge from a second living tree within 60 feet of the first tree, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second tree. Both trees must be Large or bigger.

features.name: Club.
features.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit (+6 to hit with shillelagh), reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 2 (1d4) bludgeoning damage, or 8 (1d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage with shillelagh.

features.name: Fey Charm.
features.description: The dryad targets one humanoid or beast that she can see within 30 feet of her. If the target can see the dryad, it must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or be magically charmed. The charmed creature regards the dryad as a trusted friend to be heeded and protected. Although the target isn’t under the dryad’s control, it takes the dryad’s requests or actions in the most favorable way it can. Each time the dryad or its allies do anything harmful to the target, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Otherwise, the effect lasts 24 hours or until the dryad dies, is on a different plane of existence from the target, or ends the effect as a bonus action. If a target’s saving throw is successful, the target is immune to the dryad’s Fey Charm for the next 24 hours. The dryad can have no more than one humanoid and up to three beasts charmed at a time.

features.name: Longsword.
features.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 slashing damage.

features.name: Shortbow.
features.description: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 40/160 ft., one target. Hit: 1 piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 1 minute. If its saving throw result is 5 or lower, the poisoned target falls unconscious for the same duration, or until it takes damage or another creature takes an action to shake it awake.

features.name: Heart Sight.
features.description: The sprite touches a creature and magically knows the creature’s current emotional state. If the target fails a DC 10 Charisma saving throw, the sprite also knows the creature’s alignment. Celestials, fiends, and undead automatically fail the saving throw.

features.name: Invisibility.
features.description: The sprite magically turns invisible until it attacks or casts a spell, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the sprite wears or carries is invisible with it.

features.name: Charge.
features.description: If the unicorn moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a horn attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

features.name: Innate Spellcasting.
features.description: The unicorn’s innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 14). The unicorn can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components: At will: detect evil and good, druidcraft, pass without trace; 1/day each: calm emotions, dispel evil and good, entangle

features.name: Magic Resistance.
features.description: The unicorn has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

features.name: Magic Weapons.
features.description: The unicorn’s weapon attacks are magical.

features.name: Multiattack.
features.description: The unicorn makes two attacks: one with its hooves and one with its horn.

features.name: Hooves.
features.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

features.name: Horn.
features.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage.

features.name: Healing Touch (3/Day).
features.description: The unicorn touches another creature with its horn. The target magically regains 11 (2d8 + 2) hit points. In addition, the touch removes all diseases and neutralizes all poisons afflicting the target.

features.name: Teleport (1/Day).
features.description: The unicorn magically teleports itself and up to three willing creatures it can see within 5 feet of it, along with any equipment they are wearing or carrying, to a location the unicorn is familiar with, up to 1 mile away. The unicorn can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The unicorn regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

features.name: Hooves.
features.description: The unicorn makes one attack with its hooves.

features.name: Shimmering Shield (Costs 2 Actions).
features.description: The unicorn creates a shimmering, magical field around itself or another creature it can see within 60 feet of it. The target gains a +2 bonus to AC until the end of the unicorn’s next turn.

features.name: Heal Self (Costs 3 Actions).
features.description: The unicorn magically regains 11 (2d8 + 2) hit points.

profiles.name: The Lantern Keeper
profiles.description: Begin as a classless level-zero traveler, take one action in the world, then choose how that first choice shapes your class.
profiles.prompt: What kind of traveler reaches the last light?
profiles.class_prompt: What did your first choice reveal about you?

species.label: Human
species.detail: adaptable, familiar, and hard to surprise twice
species.title: Human
species.description: A human traveler with practical hands and a face the road has not finished writing.
species.visual_prompt: human traveler, expressive face, practical layered road clothes

species.label: Mouse
species.detail: small, quick, and attentive to overlooked things
species.title: Mouse
species.description: A small mouse traveler with bright eyes, careful paws, and an instinct for the useful edge of a room.
species.visual_prompt: anthropomorphic mouse traveler, bright eyes, careful paws, small practical satchel

species.label: Badger
species.detail: sturdy, patient, and difficult to hurry
species.title: Badger
species.description: A broad badger traveler with weathered fur, a patient stance, and room for one more burden.
species.visual_prompt: anthropomorphic badger traveler, weathered fur, broad patient posture

origins.label: The Wayside Inn
origins.detail: you learned people by the state of their boots
origins.title: the Wayside Inn
origins.description: They grew up around kettle steam, wet cloaks, and stories told once the door was barred.
origins.visual_prompt: warm wayside inn details, kettle steam, patched travel cloak

origins.label: The Open Road
origins.detail: home was whichever milepost still had daylight
origins.title: the Open Road
origins.description: They learned to sleep lightly, mend straps in rain, and remember every honest milepost.
origins.visual_prompt: open road details, weathered map ribbon, rain-mended traveling gear

origins.label: The Old Chapel
origins.detail: you know which old customs still keep people safe
origins.title: the Old Chapel
origins.description: They remember soot-black rafters, small bells, and rules whose reasons were never written down.
origins.visual_prompt: old roadside chapel details, small brass bell, soot-marked cloth

choices.label: Lantern Warden
choices.detail: hold the line and bring people home
choices.calling: I keep a light burning when others lose the road.
choices.title: Lantern Warden
choices.description: A steady road-guard with a battered shield, a hooded lamp, and no habit of abandoning stragglers.

choices.label: Mothwood Guide
choices.detail: read tracks, weather, and frightened silences
choices.calling: I find the path that still has living footprints on it.
choices.title: Mothwood Guide
choices.description: A weather-wise scout carrying chalk, twine, and a map corrected more often than it is folded.

choices.label: Hedge Mender
choices.calling: I mend what the dark road tries to break.
choices.title: Hedge Mender
choices.description: A traveling fixer whose satchel holds bandages, wire, tea leaves, and exactly the wrong-sized screwdriver.
